route_observations.note: compiled route stays on the Pareto front for fast validator-bound patch work
learned route is fast but falls below the validator-heavy evidence floor
external route is slower but still Pareto-valid on evidence
hybrid route remains competitive but does not beat compiled on latency

case_reports.note: patch case shows where lower latency and valid evidence can coexist without externalization

route_observations.note: compiled route is too under-evidenced for the challenge-heavy search pack
learned route is fastest but invalid once challenge and validator costs are counted
external route owns the honest high-evidence frontier for challenge-heavy search
hybrid route stays Pareto-valid but still loses to external on correctness and evidence

case_reports.note: challenge-heavy search is the core case where lower latency is not a win once evidence posture becomes invalid

route_observations.note: compiled route remains stable but not Pareto-best on this medium-evidence trial-and-error pack
learned route stays Pareto-valid when the evidence floor is moderate rather than validator-heavy
external route stays on the frontier for maximum evidence
hybrid route balances latency and evidence most cleanly here

case_reports.note: medium-evidence trial-and-error work is where learned and hybrid routes can honestly stay on the Pareto frontier together

route_observations.note: compiled route breaks the long-loop validator thresholds
learned route stays too brittle under long-loop validator pressure
external route is the strongest evidence path but very slow
hybrid route offers the best latency/evidence compromise under the current thresholds

case_reports.note: long-loop validator work is where the hybrid route becomes justified even though external delegation still has the strongest evidence posture

compute_market_dependency_marker: compute-market remains the owner of canonical route-to-product promotion outside standalone psionic
nexus_dependency_marker: nexus remains the owner of accepted-outcome and settlement-qualified tradeoff closure outside standalone psionic
claim_boundary: this eval report is benchmark-bound product research over latency, correctness, and evidence tradeoffs. It keeps validator cost, challenge rate, and route thresholds explicit without treating the current Pareto fronts as product truth or authority closure
summary: Latency/evidence tradeoff report covers 4 cases with 3 validator-heavy cases and 2 threshold-crossing cases.
